utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan provides both (named policies with monthly costs) and a total health cost. Fully specified. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides a named contents policy with monthly cost and coverage details. Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. Plan provides a named policy with monthly cost and coverage details. Fully specified. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. Plan states none and clarifies included in core packages. This satisfies the requirement. 5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Plan provides monthly and annual costs, savings, and effective date. Fully specified. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three core items: LifeFirst Standard Physical Health Insurance, LifeFirst Standard Mental Health Insurance, PrimeHome Premium Contents Insurance, and PrimeTravel Premium Travel Insurance. In the ground-truth ratings, these correspond to: Primary Health Coverage -> physical_health -> LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 9); Primary Health Coverage -> mental_health -> LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 9); Property Protection -> home_content -> PrimeHome Premium Coverage (rating 6); Mobility Coverage -> travel_insurance -> PrimeTravel Premium Plan (rating 6). Mapping each named item accordingly yields their ratings.
